research.product: GitLab (REST API v4, CI/CD job artifacts)
research.interface: GitLab REST API v4 job artifacts endpoints

official_sources.title: Job Artifacts API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /projects/:id/jobs/:job_id/artifacts serves the zip archive with 200 or answers 404; PRIVATE-TOKEN header or job_token parameter auth; downloads from GitLab.com may redirect through a CDN (curl needs --location); POST /projects/:id/jobs/:job_id/artifacts/keep; expired artifacts are removed by scheduled cleanup

official_sources.title: Jobs API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /projects/:id/pipelines/:pipeline_id/jobs is paginated (20 per page default) and returns job objects with id, name, stage, status, artifacts[], artifacts_expire_at, web_url; job status values include created, pending, running, failed, success, canceled, canceling, skipped

official_sources.title: REST API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: base path /api/v4; PRIVATE-TOKEN request header for personal access tokens; offset pagination controlled by page/per_page with x-next-page, x-page, x-per-page, x-total, x-total-pages, x-prev-page response headers

official_sources.title: Pipelines API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /projects/:id/pipelines supports status, order_by, sort filters; pipeline status enumeration includes success as the terminal state used to select a completed pipeline

research.language_rationale: GitLab documents job-artifact download as plain REST over /api/v4 with header auth and redirect-following semantics; Python's stdlib (urllib/http.client, hashlib, zipfile, http.server for the hermetic mock) exercises exactly that documented surface with zero dependencies, so raw REST in Python is the credible integration path.

verified_facts: The GitLab REST API lives under /api/v4 and authenticates personal access tokens with the PRIVATE-TOKEN request header.
GET /projects/:id/jobs/:job_id/artifacts returns the job's artifact archive as a zip with status 200, and 404 when nothing can be served.
Artifact downloads on GitLab.com can redirect (CDN/object storage); the official docs tell curl users to pass --location, so clients must follow Location headers themselves.
Job objects returned by the Jobs API carry artifacts[] metadata and artifacts_expire_at, which is null for artifacts that do not expire.
List endpoints use offset pagination with page/per_page and expose x-next-page (empty on the final page) among the documented pagination response headers.
GitLab masks resources the token cannot see as 404, so a 404 from a project-scoped endpoint means either missing or forbidden.
Rate-limited or failed responses carry a JSON body with a message field, e.g. {"message": "404 Project Not Found"}.
